title: Systems Programming Labs (Four Modules)
summary: Four independent lab assignments covering core systems topics: a segregated-free-list memory allocator (C), two bash scripting utilities, a UNIX shell with piping and signal handling (C++), and a multi-threaded HTTP server with CGI and directory listing (C++). Each lab is independent at the cross-lab level but has an internal implementation order within its own files.
instructionPreview: This project spans four lab assignments that together cover core systems-programming topics: a custom memory allocator (C), shell scripting utilities, a UNIX shell implementation (C++), and a multi-threaded HTTP server (C++). Your working directory is `/workspace`. All project code is already present.
instruction:
This project spans four lab assignments that together cover core systems-programming topics: a custom memory allocator (C),
shell scripting utilities, a UNIX shell implementation (C++), and a multi-threaded HTTP server
(C++). Your working directory is `/workspace`. All project code is already present.

## Modules to Implement

- **lab1-src-final — Dynamic Memory Allocator** (`lab1-src-final/`): A fixed-size arena-based
  allocator using segregated free lists (59 size classes), explicit header metadata, fenceposts
  for chunk boundaries, and block splitting/coalescing. You must implement the full allocation
  and deallocation pipeline in `myMalloc.c`, including free list management helpers.
  (~1 file, ~8 functions to implement)

- **lab2-src — Shell Scripting Utilities** (`lab2-src/`): Two bash scripts that must be
  implemented. `pwcheck.sh` is a password strength scoring tool that reads a password from a
  file, validates its length, and computes a numeric score based on character class presence and
  patterns. `monitor.sh` is a process monitoring daemon that tracks CPU and memory usage of a
  given PID by reading `/proc`, generates timestamped reports, enforces a maximum report count,
  and sends email notifications when thresholds are exceeded.
  (~2 files, ~8 functions to implement)

- **lab3-src-final — UNIX Shell** (`lab3-src-final/`): A shell implementation with piping,
  I/O redirection (stdin/stdout/stderr), built-in commands (exit, setenv, unsetenv, cd,
  printenv), background process management, signal handling (SIGINT, SIGCHLD), command history
  navigation, and .shellrc sourcing. You must implement the command execution engine, the
  terminal line editor with raw-mode input handling, and supporting infrastructure.
  (~4 files, ~8 functions to implement)

- **lab5-src — HTTP Server** (`lab5-src/`): A multi-threaded HTTP server (`myhttpd.cc`) that
  serves static files, directory listings with sorting, CGI-bin execution (including loadable
  .so modules via dlopen), HTTP Basic authentication, request statistics tracking, and logging.
  It supports iterative, fork-based, thread-per-request, and thread-pool concurrency modes.
  (~1 file, ~10 functions to implement)

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called directly by the test suite (via compiled test binaries in
lab1). You must implement them with these exact signatures:

- `void * my_malloc(size_t size)` — allocate memory from the arena
- `void my_free(void * p)` — deallocate previously allocated memory
- `void * my_calloc(size_t nmemb, size_t size)` — allocate and zero-fill memory
- `void * my_realloc(void * ptr, size_t size)` — reallocate memory
- `bool verify()` — validate free list and boundary tag integrity

Build lab1 tests with: `cd /workspace/lab1-src-final && make -C tests`

moduleDag.description: Four independent lab assignments covering core systems topics: a segregated-free-list memory allocator (C), two bash scripting utilities, a UNIX shell with piping and signal handling (C++), and a multi-threaded HTTP server with CGI and directory listing (C++). Each lab is independent at the cross-lab level but has an internal implementation order within its own files.

nodes.label: Free List Primitive Helpers
nodes.description: Primitive linked-list operations on the free list sentinel array: insert_into_freelist places a block into its size-class bucket, delete_from_freelist unlinks a block by pointer, and update_freelist swaps an old header for a new one in-place (used during coalescing).

nodes.label: Allocation and Deallocation Pipeline
nodes.description: The main allocator logic: ptr_to_approp_freelist searches size-class buckets for a fitting block, split_block carves off excess and reinsertes the remainder, insert_chunk_to_freelist extends the arena via sbrk with chunk coalescing, allocate_object is the top-level allocation entry point, and deallocate_object implements boundary-tag coalescing with all three neighbor cases.

nodes.label: Password Strength Checker
nodes.description: Bash script that reads a password from a file, validates its length (6-32 chars), then computes a numeric score: starts at password length, adds +5 for special chars, digits, and alphabetic presence, and subtracts points for repeated characters and long same-class runs.

nodes.label: Process Monitor Daemon
nodes.description: Bash daemon that polls /proc/{pid}/stat and /proc/{pid}/status at a configurable interval, generates timestamped report files under reports_dir/ capped at MAXIMUM_REPORTS, converts CPU jiffies to a usage percentage using bc, and sends email via mailx when CPU or memory thresholds are exceeded.

nodes.label: SimpleCommand Data Structure
nodes.description: Implements the SimpleCommand class: destructor that frees all argument strings, insertArgument that appends to the argv vector, and print that echoes all arguments. This is the atomic pipeline unit that Command aggregates into a multi-stage command.

nodes.label: Terminal Raw-Mode Line Editor
nodes.description: Implements read_line: switches the terminal to raw mode (tty-raw-mode.c), then handles printable input, backspace, Ctrl-A/E/D for cursor movement and deletion, left/right arrow keys for in-line editing with a right-side buffer, and up/down arrows for circular history traversal. Returns the completed line on Enter.

nodes.label: Command Execution Engine
nodes.description: Implements Command: clear/print for lifecycle management, redirect to assign stdin/stdout/stderr files, and execute which handles built-in commands (exit, setenv, unsetenv, cd, printenv), sets up inter-command pipes, forks child processes with dup2-based I/O redirection, calls execvp, and waits for foreground completion or records background PIDs.

nodes.label: Shell Entry Point, Parser, and Signal Handling
nodes.description: Implements Shell::prompt (reads PROMPT env var), sigIntHandler for SIGINT (clears and re-prompts) and SIGCHLD (reaps background children with waitpid), source() to execute .shellrc at startup via yypush_buffer_state, and main() which registers signal handlers, sets $$ and $SHELL env vars, calls source(), and enters the yyparse() main loop driven by the flex/bison grammar in shell.l and shell.y.

nodes.label: HTTP Response Helpers and Sort Comparators
nodes.description: Standalone helper functions: endsWith for suffix matching used in path dispatch, writeHeader/writeFail to emit HTTP response headers, writeLink to render an HTML directory entry with icon, modification time, and file size, and six qsort comparators (sortNameA/D, sortModifiedTimeA/D, sortSizeA/D) for directory listing sort modes.

nodes.label: HTTP Server Core and Concurrency Modes
nodes.description: The server's main logic: processRequest parses an HTTP GET line and Authorization header, enforces Basic auth, dispatches to static-file serving, directory listing (with sorting), CGI-bin fork/execvp, loadable .so dlopen/dlsym, and the /stats endpoint; poolSlave and processRequestThread wrap processRequest for thread-pool and per-thread modes; main sets up the TCP socket and dispatches to iterative, fork, thread-per-request, or thread-pool mode.

edges.label: allocate_object drives insert_chunk_to_freelist which calls insert_into_freelist; ptr_to_approp_freelist calls delete_from_freelist when removing a block from its bucket; deallocate_object calls delete_from_freelist, update_freelist, and insert_into_freelist during all three coalescing cases
Command stores a vector of SimpleCommand* and accesses _arguments inside execute; SimpleCommand must be implemented before Command can be compiled or tested
The flex scanner (shell.l) calls read_line for interactive input; read-line.c must be working before the shell's parse loop can accept and process user commands

Shell::_currentCommand is a Command instance; shell.cc calls Shell::prompt and relies on Shell::_bgPIDs (populated inside Command::execute) for SIGCHLD reaping; command.cc must be complete before the shell main loop can execute commands correctly

processRequest calls endsWith for URL path dispatch, writeHeader/writeFail for responses, writeLink for directory entries, and all six sort comparators as qsort callbacks; all utility functions must exist before processRequest can serve correct responses
